These are the identity-leak\n5\t> sites — the actual goal of the umbrella effort (parent findings: `notes/bf-3ldj-findings.md`\n6\t> §5 Tier 1 + §6 data flow, `notes/bf-4bhd.md` Pattern 4).\n7\t>\n8\t> **All file:line references below were re-verified against HEAD `7846408` on 2026-07-06.**\n9\t> The four sites named in the task have **drifted** since the reference notes were written;\n10\t> the corrected (current) line numbers are authoritative and are recorded in §1.\n11\t>\n12\t> Paths: the reference notes use the module-relative path `cmd/mothership/main.go`. The\n13\t> repo-relative path (used throughout this doc) is `mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go` —\n14\t> same file.\n15\t\n16\t---\n17\t\n18\t## 0. TL;DR\n19\t\n20\t| Task site (claimed) | Verified site (HEAD `7846408`) | Boundary | Target carries identity? | Verdict |\n21\t|---|---|---|---|---|\n22\t| `main.go:5384` (E1) | **`main.go:5494`** | `fusion.Blob` peak → `sigproc.TrackedBlob` | Target **has** 6 identity fields | Left zero — **by design** (peak source is pre-identity) |\n23\t| `main.go:2213` (E2) | **`main.go:2303`** | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `automation.TrackedBlob` | Target has **none** | ❌ **LEAK — identity dropped**, no compensation |\n24\t| `main.go:2116` (E3) | **`main.go:2206`** | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `explainability.BlobSnapshot` | Target has **none** | ⚠ **LEAK — dropped at the snapshot**, but a separate `identityMap` side-channel (`main.go:2216`) partially compensates |\n25\t| `main.go:2236` (volume) | **`main.go:2326`** | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `volume.BlobPos` | Target **has** `PersonID` | ⚠ **Dropped** — field exists but conversion leaves it `\"\"` |\n26\t\n27\tThe two boundaries to fix for the umbrella goal are **E2 (automation, `:2303`)** and\n28\t**E3 (explainability, `:2206`)** — exactly as the reference notes flag, only the line\n29\tnumbers have moved (+90 to +110 lines).\n30\t\n31\t**One material correction to the reference notes** (see §3): the live 10 Hz loop does **not**\n32\trun `tracker.TrackManager`/`applyIdentity`. It runs a simple peak-associator (`blobTracker`,\n33\t`main.go:5433`) that emits `sigproc.TrackedBlob` with **identity fields permanently zero**.\n34\tResolved identity lives in a sidecar — `ble.IdentityMatcher`, queried by `blob.ID` — and is\n35\tnever written back onto the blob struct. So \"identity dropped at the conversion\" really means\n36\t\"the conversion is the place identity *could* be attached from the sidecar, and isn't.\"\n37\t\n38\t---\n39\t\n40\t## 1. The four named conversion boundaries (verified against HEAD)\n41\t\n42\tAll four live in two places: **E1** is in `(*blobTracker).track` (`main.go:5447`); **E2, E3,\n43\tvolume** are inline stages of the live 10 Hz fusion loop. For the latter three the loop source\n44\tis `blobs`, declared at `main.go:2057`:\n45\t\n46\t```go\n47\tblobs := blobTracker.track(result)   // []sigproc.TrackedBlob  (main.go:2057)\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\tSo the **source type for E2 / E3 / volume is `sigproc.TrackedBlob`** (alias of\n51\t`signal.TrackedBlob`; import at `main.go:63`: `sigproc \"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/signal\"`).\n52\t\n53\t### 1.1 E1 — `main.go:5494` — `fusion.Blob` peak → `sigproc.TrackedBlob`\n54\t\n55\t- **Enclosing func:** `(*blobTracker).track` (`main.go:5447`).\n56\t- **Source type:** `fusion.Blob` — `{X, Y, Z, Confidence}` (`internal/fusion/fusion.go:36`).\n57\t  **No identity fields** (a fusion peak is pre-identity by construction).\n58\t- **Target type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`internal/signal/processor.go:587`) — **has** 6\n59\t  identity fields (`PersonID`, `PersonLabel`, `PersonColor`, `IdentityConfidence`,\n60\t  `IdentitySource`, `Posture`).\n61\t- **Literal (`main.go:5494`):**\n62\t  ```go\n63\t  b := sigproc.TrackedBlob{\n64\t      ID:     id,           // from greedy nearest-neighbour association, NOT from the peak\n65\t      X:      pk.X,\n66\t      Y:      pk.Y,\n67\t      Z:      pk.Z,\n68\t      Weight: pk.Confidence,\n69\t  }\n70\t  // then, if a previous position exists for this id (main.go:5501-5505):\n71\t  //   b.VX = (pk.X - pb.X) / dt ; b.VY = ... ; b.VZ = ...\n72\t  ```\n73\t- **Copied:** `ID` (tracker-assigned), `X/Y/Z`, `Weight` (← peak `Confidence`); `VX/VY/VZ`\n74\t  derived from the previous frame's position of the same ID.\n75\t- **Identity dropped:** all 6 identity fields left at Go zero values.\n76\t- **Why this is \"by design\", not a leak:** the source is a pre-identity peak, and `blobTracker`\n77\t  is a pure position-associator — it has no access to BLE matches. Identity is attached\n78\t  *later* (in the sidecar; see §3). Tier 3 in `bf-3ldj` §5.\n79\t\n80\t### 1.2 E2 — `main.go:2303` — `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `automation.TrackedBlob`  ❌ LEAK\n81\t\n82\t- **Enclosing stage:** \"Stage 6: Trigger evaluation\" (`main.go:2298`), inside the\n83\t  `if automationEngine != nil {` block.\n84\t- **Source type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`b`, range var over `blobs`).\n85\t- **Target type:** `automation.TrackedBlob` (`internal/automation/engine.go:1337`):\n86\t  ```go\n87\t  type TrackedBlob struct {\n88\t      ID         int\n89\t      X, Y, Z    float64\n90\t      VX, VY, VZ float64\n91\t      Confidence float64\n92\t  }\n93\t  ```\n94\t  **The target type carries NO identity fields at all** (ID + pos + vel + Confidence only).\n95\t- **Literal (`main.go:2303`):**\n96\t  ```go\n97\t  autoBlobs[i] = automation.TrackedBlob{\n98\t      ID:         b.ID,\n99\t      X:          b.X,  Y: b.Y,  Z: b.Z,\n100\t      VX:         b.VX, VY: b.VY, VZ: b.VZ,\n101\t      Confidence: b.Weight,\n102\t  }\n103\t  ```\n104\t- **Copied:** `ID`, `X/Y/Z`, `VX/VY/VZ`, `Confidence` (← `b.Weight`).\n105\t- **Identity dropped:** all of it — and the target struct has nowhere to put it even if the\n106\t  source had it.\n107\t- **No side-channel compensation:** `automationEngine.Evaluate(autoBlobs, zoneForBlob)`\n108\t  (`main.go:2314`) is passed only a `zoneForBlob` resolver (returns zone name), **not** a\n109\t  person resolver. The engine does have a `personProvider` (wired at `main.go:3147`,\n110\t  `automationEngine.SetPersonProvider(&automationPersonAdapter{registry: bleRegistry})`),\n111\t  but it resolves `PersonName`/`PersonColor` **given a `PersonID`** (`engine.go:952`) — and\n112\t  no path supplies a `PersonID` for a blob, because `automation.TrackedBlob` has no such field\n113\t  and `Evaluate` receives only position. **Person-aware automations (\"when Alice enters…\") are\n114\t  therefore structurally blocked at this boundary.** This is the highest-value fix.\n115\t\n116\t### 1.3 E3 — `main.go:2206` — `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `explainability.BlobSnapshot`  ⚠ LEAK (partly compensated)\n117\t\n118\t- **Enclosing stage:** \"Stage 2\" explainability block (`main.go:2160`,\n119\t  `if explainabilityHandler != nil {`).\n120\t- **Source type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`blob`, range var over `blobs`).\n121\t- **Target type:** `explainability.BlobSnapshot` (`internal/explainability/handler.go:95`):\n122\t  ```go\n123\t  type BlobSnapshot struct {\n124\t      ID         int\n125\t      X, Y, Z    float64\n126\t      Confidence float64\n127\t      Weight     float64 // Peak height in the grid\n128\t  }\n129\t  ```\n130\t  **The target type carries NO identity fields.**\n131\t- **Literal (`main.go:2206`):**\n132\t  ```go\n133\t  blobSnapshots = append(blobSnapshots, explainability.BlobSnapshot{\n134\t      ID:         blob.ID,\n135\t      X:          blob.X,  Y: blob.Y,  Z: blob.Z,\n136\t      Confidence: blob.Weight,\n137\t  })\n138\t  ```\n139\t- **Copied:** `ID`, `X/Y/Z`, `Confidence` (← `blob.Weight`). (`BlobSnapshot.Weight` — peak\n140\t  height — is left `0`; no grid snapshot is available in this path, see `main.go:2235`.)\n141\t- **Identity dropped:** person/color/source — none carried by the snapshot.\n142\t- **Side-channel compensation (important):** immediately after the snapshot loop, a separate\n143\t  `identityMap` is built from the sidecar and passed alongside the snapshots\n144\t  (`main.go:2215-2236`):\n145\t  ```go\n146\t  identityMap := make(map[int]*explainability.BLEMatch)\n147\t  for _, blob := range blobs {\n148\t      match := identityMatcher.GetMatch(blob.ID)\n149\t      if match != nil && match.PersonID != \"\" { ... identityMap[blob.ID] = &explainability.BLEMatch{...} }\n150\t  }\n151\t  explainabilityHandler.UpdateBlobs(blobSnapshots, linkStates, gridSnapshot, identityMap)\n152\t  ```\n153\t  So the \"Why?\" overlay **does** receive identity — via the `identityMap` argument keyed by\n154\t  `blob.ID`, not via the `BlobSnapshot`. The snapshot struct itself remains identity-free; if\n155\t  `BlobSnapshot` is ever consumed in isolation (without `identityMap`), the person name is\n156\t  lost. The clean fix is to put identity on `BlobSnapshot` and drop the parallel map.\n157\t\n158\t### 1.4 volume — `main.go:2326` — `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `volume.BlobPos`  ⚠ Dropped\n159\t\n160\t- **Enclosing stage:** \"Stage 6: Trigger evaluation\" (`main.go:2322`),\n161\t  `if volumeTriggersHandler != nil {`.\n162\t- **Source type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`blob`, range var over `blobs`).\n163\t- **Target type:** `volume.BlobPos` (`internal/volume/shape.go:1080`):\n164\t  ```go\n165\t  type BlobPos struct {\n166\t      ID       int\n167\t      PersonID string   // ← identity field EXISTS on the target\n168\t      X, Y, Z  float64\n169\t  }\n170\t  ```\n171\t  **The target type DOES carry an identity field (`PersonID`).**\n172\t- **Literal (`main.go:2326`):**\n173\t  ```go\n174\t  volumeBlobs[i] = volume.BlobPos{\n175\t      ID: blob.ID,\n176\t      X:  blob.X,  Y: blob.Y,  Z: blob.Z,\n177\t  }\n178\t  ```\n179\t- **Copied:** `ID`, `X/Y/Z` only.\n180\t- **Identity dropped:** `PersonID` — the field is **present** on `volume.BlobPos` but the\n181\t  conversion **does not populate it** (left `\"\"`). Velocity is also not copied (not carried by\n182\t  `BlobPos`). Because the field already exists, this is the cheapest boundary to repair: a\n183\t  single `PersonID: <from sidecar>` line would unblock person-filtered volume triggers\n184\t  (`condition_params.person`).\n185\t\n186\t---\n187\t\n188\t## 2. Complete enumeration of blob-conversion loops in main.go\n189\t\n190\tAcceptance criterion: *\"Every main.go blob-conversion loop enumerated with source type,\n191\ttarget type, copied fields.\"* Beyond the four named boundaries, the live loop projects the\n192\ttracked blob into several other shapes. Full list (HEAD `7846408`):\n193\t\n194\t| # | main.go:line | Source | Target type | Fields copied | Identity? |\n195\t|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n196\t| 1 | `:2087` | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`blob`) | `map[string]interface{}` (detection-event JSON detail) | `x,y,z,vx,vy,vz,confidence,posture` | person passed **separately** as `personID` arg to `eventsHandler.LogEvent` (`:2100`) — not in the map |\n197\t| 2 | `:2113` | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`b`) | anon struct `{ID,X,Y,Z,Weight}` → `identityMatcher.UpdateBlobs` | `ID,X,Y,Z,Weight` | n/a — matcher is the identity **producer**, not consumer; Material correction to the reference data-flow diagram\n218\t\n219\t`bf-3ldj-findings.md` §6 draws the live path as:\n220\t\n221\t```\n222\tfusion.Blob peak → sigproc.TrackedBlob [E1] → tracker.Blob [A1 tracker.go:162]\n223\t   → TrackManager.UpdateWithIdentity [A3] ✅ IDENTITY ATTACHED → GetAllBlobs [B1/B2] → consumers\n224\t```\n225\t\n226\t**That is not the live path in HEAD `7846408`.** Verified facts:\n227\t\n228\t1. **`tracker.Tracker` / `tracker.TrackManager` is not wired into `main.go` at all** — zero\n229\t   references in `cmd/mothership/main.go` (non-test). The only identity-field writes in the tree are on\n238\t   `tracker.Blob` inside `internal/tracker/identity.go` (the unused-in-live-path 3D tracker).\n239\t4. **Resolved identity lives in a sidecar:** `ble.IdentityMatcher` (`main.go:799`), queried by\n240\t   `blob.ID` via `identityMatcher.GetMatch(blob.ID)` (e.g. `main.go:2077, 2138, 2218, 2267,\n241\t   2381`). It is never written back onto the blob struct.\n242\t\n243\t**Corrected live data flow:**\n244\t\n245\t```\n246\tfusion.Engine.Fuse ──fusion.Blob peaks──▶  (NO identity)\n247\t   [fusion.go:260]                            │\n248\t                                              ▼\n249\tblobTracker.track ──sigproc.TrackedBlob──▶  (6 identity fields exist, ALL ZERO)\n250\t   [E1 main.go:5494]                          │  ← identity never written onto the struct\n251\t                                              ▼\n252\tpm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs) ──blobs: []sigproc.TrackedBlob──▶  every consumer reads ZERO identity\n253\t   [main.go:2057-2058]                        │\n254\t                                              │   resolved identity held SEPARATELY in:\n255\t                                              │   ble.IdentityMatcher  (main.go:799)\n256\t                                              │   queried per-blob by ID via GetMatch()\n257\t                                              ▼\n258\t   ┌──────────────┬───────────────┬───────────────────┬────────────────┬──────────────────┐\n259\t   ▼              ▼               ▼                   ▼                ▼                  ▼\n260\t analytics      explainability   automation          volume           identityMatcher    fall-detect\n261\t [:2271]        [:2206 E3]       [:2303 E2]          [:2326]          [:2113]            [:2288]\n262\t ✅ pulls       ⚠ snapshot       ❌ target has       ⚠ PersonID       (sidecar: the       (anon struct;\n263\t   PersonID        drops person     NO identity         field exists     identity SOURCE)   Posture field\n264\t   from sidecar    BUT identity      field; no          but left \"\"      — not a leak,      also not set)\n265\t   → correct       Map side-         side-channel        — cheap to       this is where\n266\t                  channel (         compensation        fix              identity is made)\n267\t                  :2216) partly\n268\t                  compensates\n269\t```\n270\t\n271\t**Implication for the fix:** because identity is in the sidecar (not on the blob), every\n272\tidentity-aware conversion boundary must call `identityMatcher.GetMatch(blob.ID)` at the\n273\tconversion site — exactly as `analytics.TrackUpdate` already does (`main.go:2267-2279`).\n274\tMerely adding fields to the target structs is insufficient; the population step is the actual\n275\tfix. The two flagged boundaries:\n276\t\n277\t- **E2 (`:2303`, automation):** add identity fields to `automation.TrackedBlob`\n278\t  (`engine.go:1337`) **and** populate them from the sidecar in the loop. Without this,\n279\t  `automationEngine.personProvider` can never fire (it needs a `PersonID` nothing supplies).\n280\t- **E3 (`:2206`, explainability):** add identity fields to `explainability.BlobSnapshot`\n281\t  (`handler.go:95`), populate from the sidecar, and fold the parallel `identityMap`\n282\t  (`main.go:2216`) into the snapshot so the \"Why?\" overlay has one identity source.\n283\t- **volume (`:2326`):** the `PersonID` field already exists — just populate it from the\n284\t  sidecar (one line) to enable person-filtered volume triggers.\n285\t\n286\t---\n287\t\n288\t## 4. These are the identity-leak\n> sites — the actual goal of the umbrella effort (parent findings: `notes/bf-3ldj-findings.md`\n> §5 Tier 1 + §6 data flow, `notes/bf-4bhd.md` Pattern 4).\n>\n> **All file:line references below were re-verified against HEAD `7846408` on 2026-07-06.**\n> The four sites named in the task have **drifted** since the reference notes were written;\n> the corrected (current) line numbers are authoritative and are recorded in §1.\n>\n> Paths: the reference notes use the module-relative path `cmd/mothership/main.go`. The\n> repo-relative path (used throughout this doc) is `mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go` —\n> same file.\n\n---\n\n## 0. TL;DR\n\n| Task site (claimed) | Verified site (HEAD `7846408`) | Boundary | Target carries identity? | Verdict |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `main.go:5384` (E1) | **`main.go:5494`** | `fusion.Blob` peak → `sigproc.TrackedBlob` | Target **has** 6 identity fields | Left zero — **by design** (peak source is pre-identity) |\n| `main.go:2213` (E2) | **`main.go:2303`** | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `automation.TrackedBlob` | Target has **none** | ❌ **LEAK — identity dropped**, no compensation |\n| `main.go:2116` (E3) | **`main.go:2206`** | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `explainability.BlobSnapshot` | Target has **none** | ⚠ **LEAK — dropped at the snapshot**, but a separate `identityMap` side-channel (`main.go:2216`) partially compensates |\n| `main.go:2236` (volume) | **`main.go:2326`** | `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `volume.BlobPos` | Target **has** `PersonID` | ⚠ **Dropped** — field exists but conversion leaves it `\"\"` |\n\nThe two boundaries to fix for the umbrella goal are **E2 (automation, `:2303`)** and\n**E3 (explainability, `:2206`)** — exactly as the reference notes flag, only the line\nnumbers have moved (+90 to +110 lines).\n\n**One material correction to the reference notes** (see §3): the live 10 Hz loop does **not**\nrun `tracker.TrackManager`/`applyIdentity`. It runs a simple peak-associator (`blobTracker`,\n`main.go:5433`) that emits `sigproc.TrackedBlob` with **identity fields permanently zero**.\nResolved identity lives in a sidecar — `ble.IdentityMatcher`, queried by `blob.ID` — and is\nnever written back onto the blob struct. So \"identity dropped at the conversion\" really means\n\"the conversion is the place identity *could* be attached from the sidecar, and isn't.\"\n\n---\n\n## 1. The four named conversion boundaries (verified against HEAD)\n\nAll four live in two places: **E1** is in `(*blobTracker).track` (`main.go:5447`); **E2, E3,\nvolume** are inline stages of the live 10 Hz fusion loop. For the latter three the loop source\nis `blobs`, declared at `main.go:2057`:\n\n```go\nblobs := blobTracker.track(result)   // []sigproc.TrackedBlob  (main.go:2057)\n```\n\nSo the **source type for E2 / E3 / volume is `sigproc.TrackedBlob`** (alias of\n`signal.TrackedBlob`; import at `main.go:63`: `sigproc \"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/signal\"`).\n\n### 1.1 E1 — `main.go:5494` — `fusion.Blob` peak → `sigproc.TrackedBlob`\n\n- **Enclosing func:** `(*blobTracker).track` (`main.go:5447`).\n- **Source type:** `fusion.Blob` — `{X, Y, Z, Confidence}` (`internal/fusion/fusion.go:36`).\n  **No identity fields** (a fusion peak is pre-identity by construction).\n- **Target type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`internal/signal/processor.go:587`) — **has** 6\n  identity fields (`PersonID`, `PersonLabel`, `PersonColor`, `IdentityConfidence`,\n  `IdentitySource`, `Posture`).\n- **Literal (`main.go:5494`):**\n  ```go\n  b := sigproc.TrackedBlob{\n      ID:     id,           // from greedy nearest-neighbour association, NOT from the peak\n      X:      pk.X,\n      Y:      pk.Y,\n      Z:      pk.Z,\n      Weight: pk.Confidence,\n  }\n  // then, if a previous position exists for this id (main.go:5501-5505):\n  //   b.VX = (pk.X - pb.X) / dt ; b.VY = ... ; b.VZ = ...\n  ```\n- **Copied:** `ID` (tracker-assigned), `X/Y/Z`, `Weight` (← peak `Confidence`); `VX/VY/VZ`\n  derived from the previous frame's position of the same ID.\n- **Identity dropped:** all 6 identity fields left at Go zero values.\n- **Why this is \"by design\", not a leak:** the source is a pre-identity peak, and `blobTracker`\n  is a pure position-associator — it has no access to BLE matches. Identity is attached\n  *later* (in the sidecar; see §3). Tier 3 in `bf-3ldj` §5.\n\n### 1.2 E2 — `main.go:2303` — `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `automation.TrackedBlob`  ❌ LEAK\n\n- **Enclosing stage:** \"Stage 6: Trigger evaluation\" (`main.go:2298`), inside the\n  `if automationEngine != nil {` block.\n- **Source type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`b`, range var over `blobs`).\n- **Target type:** `automation.TrackedBlob` (`internal/automation/engine.go:1337`):\n  ```go\n  type TrackedBlob struct {\n      ID         int\n      X, Y, Z    float64\n      VX, VY, VZ float64\n      Confidence float64\n  }\n  ```\n  **The target type carries NO identity fields at all** (ID + pos + vel + Confidence only).\n- **Literal (`main.go:2303`):**\n  ```go\n  autoBlobs[i] = automation.TrackedBlob{\n      ID:         b.ID,\n      X:          b.X,  Y: b.Y,  Z: b.Z,\n      VX:         b.VX, VY: b.VY, VZ: b.VZ,\n      Confidence: b.Weight,\n  }\n  ```\n- **Copied:** `ID`, `X/Y/Z`, `VX/VY/VZ`, `Confidence` (← `b.Weight`).\n- **Identity dropped:** all of it — and the target struct has nowhere to put it even if the\n  source had it.\n- **No side-channel compensation:** `automationEngine.Evaluate(autoBlobs, zoneForBlob)`\n  (`main.go:2314`) is passed only a `zoneForBlob` resolver (returns zone name), **not** a\n  person resolver. The engine does have a `personProvider` (wired at `main.go:3147`,\n  `automationEngine.SetPersonProvider(&automationPersonAdapter{registry: bleRegistry})`),\n  but it resolves `PersonName`/`PersonColor` **given a `PersonID`** (`engine.go:952`) — and\n  no path supplies a `PersonID` for a blob, because `automation.TrackedBlob` has no such field\n  and `Evaluate` receives only position. **Person-aware automations (\"when Alice enters…\") are\n  therefore structurally blocked at this boundary.** This is the highest-value fix.\n\n### 1.3 E3 — `main.go:2206` — `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `explainability.BlobSnapshot`  ⚠ LEAK (partly compensated)\n\n- **Enclosing stage:** \"Stage 2\" explainability block (`main.go:2160`,\n  `if explainabilityHandler != nil {`).\n- **Source type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`blob`, range var over `blobs`).\n- **Target type:** `explainability.BlobSnapshot` (`internal/explainability/handler.go:95`):\n  ```go\n  type BlobSnapshot struct {\n      ID         int\n      X, Y, Z    float64\n      Confidence float64\n      Weight     float64 // Peak height in the grid\n  }\n  ```\n  **The target type carries NO identity fields.**\n- **Literal (`main.go:2206`):**\n  ```go\n  blobSnapshots = append(blobSnapshots, explainability.BlobSnapshot{\n      ID:         blob.ID,\n      X:          blob.X,  Y: blob.Y,  Z: blob.Z,\n      Confidence: blob.Weight,\n  })\n  ```\n- **Copied:** `ID`, `X/Y/Z`, `Confidence` (← `blob.Weight`). (`BlobSnapshot.Weight` — peak\n  height — is left `0`; no grid snapshot is available in this path, see `main.go:2235`.)\n- **Identity dropped:** person/color/source — none carried by the snapshot.\n- **Side-channel compensation (important):** immediately after the snapshot loop, a separate\n  `identityMap` is built from the sidecar and passed alongside the snapshots\n  (`main.go:2215-2236`):\n  ```go\n  identityMap := make(map[int]*explainability.BLEMatch)\n  for _, blob := range blobs {\n      match := identityMatcher.GetMatch(blob.ID)\n      if match != nil && match.PersonID != \"\" { ... identityMap[blob.ID] = &explainability.BLEMatch{...} }\n  }\n  explainabilityHandler.UpdateBlobs(blobSnapshots, linkStates, gridSnapshot, identityMap)\n  ```\n  So the \"Why?\" overlay **does** receive identity — via the `identityMap` argument keyed by\n  `blob.ID`, not via the `BlobSnapshot`. The snapshot struct itself remains identity-free; if\n  `BlobSnapshot` is ever consumed in isolation (without `identityMap`), the person name is\n  lost. The clean fix is to put identity on `BlobSnapshot` and drop the parallel map.\n\n### 1.4 volume — `main.go:2326` — `sigproc.TrackedBlob` → `volume.BlobPos`  ⚠ Dropped\n\n- **Enclosing stage:** \"Stage 6: Trigger evaluation\" (`main.go:2322`),\n  `if volumeTriggersHandler != nil {`.\n- **Source type:** `sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`blob`, range var over `blobs`).\n- **Target type:** `volume.BlobPos` (`internal/volume/shape.go:1080`):\n  ```go\n  type BlobPos struct {\n      ID       int\n      PersonID string   // ← identity field EXISTS on the target\n      X, Y, Z  float64\n  }\n  ```\n  **The target type DOES carry an identity field (`PersonID`).**\n- **Literal (`main.go:2326`):**\n  ```go\n  volumeBlobs[i] = volume.BlobPos{\n      ID: blob.ID,\n      X:  blob.X,  Y: blob.Y,  Z: blob.Z,\n  }\n  ```\n- **Copied:** `ID`, `X/Y/Z` only.\n- **Identity dropped:** `PersonID` — the field is **present** on `volume.BlobPos` but the\n  conversion **does not populate it** (left `\"\"`). Velocity is also not copied (not carried by\n  `BlobPos`). Because the field already exists, this is the cheapest boundary to repair: a\n  single `PersonID: <from sidecar>` line would unblock person-filtered volume triggers\n  (`condition_params.person`).\n\n---\n\n## 2. Complete enumeration of blob-conversion loops in main.go\n\nAcceptance criterion: *\"Every main.go blob-conversion loop enumerated with source type,\ntarget type, copied fields.\"* Beyond the four named boundaries, the live loop projects the\ntracked blob into several other shapes. Material correction to the reference data-flow diagram\n\n`bf-3ldj-findings.md` §6 draws the live path as:\n\n```\nfusion.Blob peak → sigproc.TrackedBlob [E1] → tracker.Blob [A1 tracker.go:162]\n   → TrackManager.UpdateWithIdentity [A3] ✅ IDENTITY ATTACHED → GetAllBlobs [B1/B2] → consumers\n```\n\n**That is not the live path in HEAD `7846408`.** Verified facts:\n\n1. **`tracker.Tracker` / `tracker.TrackManager` is not wired into `main.go` at all** — zero\n   references in `cmd/mothership/main.go` (non-test). The `applyIdentity` / `clearIdentity`\n   identity machinery in `internal/tracker/identity.go:164-185` therefore never runs in the\n   live loop. (grep for `tracker\\.\\|TrackManager` in main.go returns nothing.)\n2. The live loop uses `blobTracker` (`main.go:5433`, a hand-rolled greedy nearest-neighbour\n   associator) whose `track()` returns `[]sigproc.TrackedBlob` (`main.go:5447`).\n3. **`sigproc.TrackedBlob`'s 6 identity fields are never written in any live path** — grep\n   across `internal/` and `cmd/` (non-test) for `\\.PersonLabel =` / `\\.PersonID =` /\n   `\\.PersonColor =` / `\\.IdentityConfidence =` / `\\.IdentitySource =` finds **zero** writes\n   to a `sigproc.TrackedBlob`. The only identity-field writes in the tree are on\n   `tracker.Blob` inside `internal/tracker/identity.go` (the unused-in-live-path 3D tracker).\n4. **Resolved identity lives in a sidecar:** `ble.IdentityMatcher` (`main.go:799`), queried by\n   `blob.ID` via `identityMatcher.GetMatch(blob.ID)` (e.g. `main.go:2077, 2138, 2218, 2267,\n   2381`). It is never written back onto the blob struct.\n\n**Corrected live data flow:**\n\n```\nfusion.Engine.Fuse ──fusion.Blob peaks──▶  (NO identity)\n   [fusion.go:260]                            │\n                                              ▼\nblobTracker.track ──sigproc.TrackedBlob──▶  (6 identity fields exist, ALL ZERO)\n   [E1 main.go:5494]                          │  ← identity never written onto the struct\n                                              ▼\npm.SetTrackedBlobs(blobs) ──blobs: []sigproc.TrackedBlob──▶  every consumer reads ZERO identity\n   [main.go:2057-2058]                        │\n                                              │   resolved identity held SEPARATELY in:\n                                              │   ble.IdentityMatcher  (main.go:799)\n                                              │   queried per-blob by ID via GetMatch()\n                                              ▼\n   ┌──────────────┬───────────────┬───────────────────┬────────────────┬──────────────────┐\n   ▼              ▼               ▼                   ▼                ▼                  ▼\n analytics      explainability   automation          volume           identityMatcher    fall-detect\n [:2271]        [:2206 E3]       [:2303 E2]          [:2326]          [:2113]            [:2288]\n ✅ pulls       ⚠ snapshot       ❌ target has       ⚠ PersonID       (sidecar: the       (anon struct;\n   PersonID        drops person     NO identity         field exists     identity SOURCE)   Posture field\n   from sidecar    BUT identity      field; no          but left \"\"      — not a leak,      also not set)\n   → correct       Map side-         side-channel        — cheap to       this is where\n                  channel (         compensation        fix              identity is made)\n                  :2216) partly\n                  compensates\n```\n\n**Implication for the fix:** because identity is in the sidecar (not on the blob), every\nidentity-aware conversion boundary must call `identityMatcher.GetMatch(blob.ID)` at the\nconversion site — exactly as `analytics.TrackUpdate` already does (`main.go:2267-2279`).\nMerely adding fields to the target structs is insufficient; the population step is the actual\nfix. The two flagged boundaries:\n\n- **E2 (`:2303`, automation):** add identity fields to `automation.TrackedBlob`\n  (`engine.go:1337`) **and** populate them from the sidecar in the loop. Without this,\n  `automationEngine.personProvider` can never fire (it needs a `PersonID` nothing supplies).\n- **E3 (`:2206`, explainability):** add identity fields to `explainability.BlobSnapshot`\n  (`handler.go:95`), populate from the sidecar, and fold the parallel `identityMap`\n  (`main.go:2216`) into the snapshot so the \"Why?\" overlay has one identity source.\n- **volume (`:2326`):** the `PersonID` field already exists — just populate it from the\n  sidecar (one line) to enable person-filtered volume triggers.\n\n---\n\n## 4. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_120d26218c6c4399bc504651","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\n3\tdescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: reference\n7\t  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n11\t\n12\t**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n15\t1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n16\t2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n17\t3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n18\t   ```python\n19\t   import sqlite3, re\n20\t   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n21\t   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n22\t   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n23\t   con.execute('BEGIN')\n24\t   for rid, ts in rows:\n25\t       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n26\t           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n27\t                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n28\t   con.commit()\n29\t   ```\n30\t   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n31\t4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\n32\tNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n33\t\n34\t**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n35\t```bash\n36\tSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n37\t$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n38\t```\n39\t(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n40\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0d2f675a-efdb-473a-a457-0a2b98965278","uuid":"21b7ca57-6b3f-452b-a5c1-12ba050c3b0c","timestamp":"2026-07-06T19:22:44.480Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339.md","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n```bash\nSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n```\n(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}
